findings.requirement: UX: column priority — identification + key rates (reads/writes) first; less-critical columns (writebacks, reuses, fsyncs, stats_age) move to the tail and clip first
findings.detail: The user-spec UX-behavior section explicitly states that less-critical columns (writebacks, reuses, fsyncs, stats_age) should trail and be truncated first on narrow terminals. The tech-spec Data Models count-screen table places writebacks at idx 10 (before hits/evictions/reuses), not at the tail; only stats_age is last. The tail-ordering intent (writebacks/reuses/fsyncs deprioritized after reads/writes) is not reflected in the concrete column order. Risk 2 / risk-clip is acknowledged but the spec does not state the columns were deliberately ordered to push the named low-priority ones to the end. Minor: ordering is a layout detail and the clip behavior itself is standard, but the explicit user requirement about WHICH columns trail is only partially honored.

findings.requirement: Scenario 3 / track_io_timing=off: time screen shows a cmdline hint 'track_io_timing is off — timings unavailable'
findings.detail: User-spec scenario 3 and AC require a cmdline hint when track_io_timing is off. Tech-spec Decision 9 implements this as a STATIC always-present Msg ('pg_stat_io timings (require track_io_timing=on)') shown on every switch rather than conditionally when the GUC is off. This is a deliberate, justified design change (avoids per-sample inspection) and is documented as an alternative-considered, so it is acceptable — but the delivered behavior diverges from the literal user-spec wording (conditional hint vs always-on hint). Flagged as partial, not a gap, because the user intent (DBA is told timings may be zero) is satisfied.

summary: 14/15 requirements covered, 0 gaps, 0 missing, 2 partials (both minor). Strong bidirectional traceability: every user story (US1-US4), all 8 user-spec acceptance criteria, all 3 scenarios, and all constraints (PG16+ only, NotRecordable/top-first, two sub-screens, io_key composite matching, coalesce NULL-safety, version-branched KiB, Q-no-reset help note) map to tech-spec Decisions 1-11 and Tasks 1-4. No scope creep: every tech-spec element traces to a requirement; the added PostgresV15/16/17/18 constants and io_test.go are justified engineering enablers. No overengineering (two sub-screens and synthetic io_key are forced by pgcenter's no-horizontal-scroll and single-column UniqueKey constraints, both proven precedents; no speculative abstractions; tasks are brief scope descriptions). No underengineering (NULL-safety via coalesce + dedicated test, SQL-side empty-row filter, version-gate error path, integer KiB division, composite-key correctness test all present; error/boundary handling is explicit). Solution and Architecture sections have real technical substance (concrete files, selectors, diff pipeline, version branches). No critical findings -> PASS. The two partials are minor layout/wording divergences worth a glance during implementation but do not block.

traceability.US-1: reads/writes by backend_type x con: covered — count screen, 3 separate sortable dimension columns (Decision 2), default sort by reads (Decision 11), per-column / filter
traceability.US-2: WAL-IO (object=wal) on PG18, gone: covered — Decision 3 PG18 branch returns object='wal' rows; integration test gates it (Testing Strategy)
traceability.US-3: high evictions/reuses for shared_b: covered — count screen includes evictions (idx12) and reuses (idx13) columns
traceability.US-4: read/write/fsync latency by source: covered — time screen (Decision 1) read_time/write_time/fsync_time + Decision 9 hint
traceability.AC-2: per-interval rates, counters diffe: covered — Decision 4, DiffIntvl [4,14]/[4,8], stats_age outside diff
traceability.AC-3: all-NULL/0 rows hidden via SQL WHE: covered — Decision 5 count-based WHERE reused on time screen
traceability.AC-4: PG16/17 and PG18 correct KiB; PG18: covered — Decision 3 + Decision 6 integer division; integration matrix gate
traceability.AC-5: PG14/15 not-supported message, no: covered — MinRequiredVersion=PostgresV16 + collector VersionOK gate (Architecture How-it-works)
traceability.AC-6: track_io_timing=off time screen sh: partial — Decision 9 static hint instead of conditional (minor, justified)
traceability.AC-7: deltas correct when rows share bac: covered — Decision 2 synthetic io_key as UniqueKey + risk-table integration assertion
traceability.AC-8: help documents Q does not reset pg: covered — Task 3 help line, Decision in Architecture (top/help.go note)
